canonical_definition: The provided literature presents AUTO-J in two distinct ways: as a specific generative judge model and as a benchmark for evaluating other models. Several papers define it as a 13B-parameter generative model that functions as an automated evaluator. In this capacity, it is used to operationalize the construct of `Helpfulness` by rating model responses on a numeric scale. One paper also groups it with other LLM judges used to 'assess the distance between reference and output' ('SePer: Measure Retrieval Utility Through The Lens Of Semantic Perplexity Reduction'). Alternatively, other work treats AUTO-J as a benchmark for the 'meta-evaluation of LLM judges' ('Trust or Escalate: LLM Judges with Provable Guarantees for Human Agreement') and for evaluating reward models. When used as a benchmark, it is sometimes deployed alongside other instruments like RewardBench and MT-Bench. A single paper also suggests a relationship where AUTO-J is used to generate `Critique`.
papers: Advancing LLM Reasoning Generalists with Preference Trees
SePer: Measure Retrieval Utility Through The Lens Of Semantic Perplexity Reduction
Towards Federated RLHF with Aggregated Client Preference for LLMs
Trust or Escalate: LLM Judges with Provable Guarantees for Human Agreement
CriticEval: Evaluating Large-scale Language Model as Critic

definitions.text: A 13B-parameter generative judge model used as an automated evaluator to rate the helpfulness of model responses on a numeric scale.
A curated benchmark for meta-evaluation of LLM judges.
definitions.paper: Trust or Escalate: LLM Judges with Provable Guarantees for Human Agreement

definitions.text: An automated judge benchmark used to evaluate reward models, particularly on their correlation with human expert judgments.
definitions.paper: Advancing LLM Reasoning Generalists with Preference Trees
